content.problemStatement:
Chef is on his way to become the new big bull of the stock market but is a bit weak at calculating whether he made a profit or a loss on his deal.

Given that Chef bought the stock at value XXX and sold it at value YYY. Help him calculate whether he made a profit, loss, or was it a neutral deal.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of a single line of input containing two space-separated integers XXX and YYY, denoting the value at which Chef bought and sold the stock respectively.


Output Format
For each test case, output `PROFIT` if Chef made a profit on the deal, `LOSS` if Chef incurred a loss on the deal, and `NEUTRAL` otherwise.


The checker is case-insensitive so answers like `pROfiT`, `profit`, and `PROFIT` would be considered the same.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two space‑separated integers X and Y, the buying price and the selling price respectively.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing one of the strings: PROFIT if Y > X, LOSS if Y < X, or NEUTRAL if Y == X.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: 4 > 2 → LOSS. Test case 2: 8 = 8 → NEUTRAL. Test case 3: 3 < 4 → PROFIT. Test case 4: 2 > 1 → LOSS.
Buying price 5, selling price 10, so Y > X → PROFIT.
Buying and selling prices are equal, therefore the deal is NEUTRAL.

solution.approach: For each test case read X and Y. Compare the two values: if Y is greater than X the transaction yields a profit, if Y is smaller it yields a loss, otherwise it is neutral. Output the corresponding word. The algorithm runs in O(T) time and O(1) extra space.
